folio.label: 138v
folio.l: Thow art a Squyer , and he is a knygút
But f f god forbede , for his blisful mygút
But if a Clerk , coude doon a gentil dede
As wel as any of ©ow , it is no drede
¢ Sire I relesse the , thy thousand pound
As thow rigút now , were cropen out of the ground
Ne neuäe er now , ne haddest knowen me
For Sire , I wol nat take , a peny of the
For al my craft , ne nougút for my trauaille
Thow hast I paid wel , for my vitaille
It is I now , and farewel haue good day
And toke his hors , & fortú he gotú his wey
Lordynges this questiou~ , than wil I axe now
Whicú was the moost free , as thynketú ©ow
Now telletú me , or that ©e ferthere wende
I can namore , my tale is at an ende ¢ Amen

note.notetxt: FRthe explicit is written in the same ink. Large writing Bartard Anglicana, very similar to Hg.ODR
